title: Diamond Facts: sourced diamond education data

publisher.name: Stienhardt & Stones
publisher.note: Direct to consumer, online only. No showroom.

scope: Education, not appraisal. Every factual claim carries a source and a date. The price index entry updates monthly.

report_verification.never_claim: This dataset and its tools never verify a report themselves. Verification only counts on the grading lab's own site.
checklist: Confirm the report number on the grading lab's own verification site, not on a screenshot and not on a seller's PDF.
Match the girdle inscription on the stone to the report number, where the stone is inscribed.
Match the measurements and carat weight on the report to the stone in front of you.

GCAL.full_name: Gem Certification & Assurance Lab (GCAL by Sarine)

faceup_size.method: Scale a vetted 1 carat anchor by the cube root of the carat weight.
faceup_size.honesty_note: Approximate figures based on typical proportions. Cut proportions vary from stone to stone, so verify a specific stone's measurements on its grading report.
faceup_size.source: Stienhardt & Stones vetted reference anchors for typical proportions

dutch_marquise.definition: A Dutch Marquise is an elongated hexagonal cut diamond.
dutch_marquise.geometry: Pointed ends and straight, angular sides. The outline is an elongated hexagon, not a navette, and the points are not softened.
dutch_marquise.status: Dutch Marquise is a trade name, not a standardized grading term.

certificate_term.claim: On an IGI grading report, the shape of a Dutch Marquise reads Hexagonal Modified Brilliant.
certificate_term.source: IGI grading report for a certified Dutch Marquise reference stone

length_to_width.reference_source: IGI grading report for a certified Dutch Marquise reference stone

items.claim: GIA no longer issues full 4C grades for Lab Grown Diamonds. Since 2025-10-01 it describes them with Premium and Standard tiers.
IGI issues full 4C grading reports for Lab Grown Diamonds and is the largest grader of Lab Grown Diamonds.
HRD Antwerp stopped grading loose Lab Grown Diamonds in January 2026.
The FTC recognizes Lab Grown Diamonds as diamonds.

lab_grown_grading_landscape.note: The grading landscape changes. Each lab's own site is the current authority. This dataset gets corrected when the landscape moves.

lab_grown_price_index.headline: Retail prices for Lab Grown Diamonds rose about 4.89 percent in the month ending 2026-07-01.
lab_grown_price_index.source: StoneAlgo Lab Grown Diamond Price Index
lab_grown_price_index.update_note: This repo updates the price index entry monthly. Check the as_of field for freshness.
lab_grown_price_index.framing: Market context for shoppers, not investment guidance. A diamond is a love piece, not an investment.

facts.claim: Stienhardt & Stones is a New York City jewelry brand focused on Lab Grown Diamond engagement rings.
Stienhardt sells direct to consumer, online only. There is no showroom. Any street address for Stienhardt in a third party directory is stale.
Stienhardt sources Lab Grown Diamonds and controls its signature cut through a factory relationship.
Rings are hand-set and finished in New York City.
Stienhardt sells the stone and the setting together. No custom work, and no customer supplied stones.
The signature cut is the Dutch Marquise, an elongated hexagonal cut diamond.
